input_dict.agent_role: Produce one compact second-hop retrieval query targeting the missing bridge or supporting evidence.
input_dict.agent_function: Pipeline position: after summary1 and before second-hop retrieval. Inputs are the original question and the first-hop intermediate state. Functional responsibility: produce one focused retrieval query that is likely to recover the missing evidence needed by the downstream agents. Query quality is determined by evidence recovery, not by whether the query itself resembles or contains the final answer.
input_dict.native_output_contract: The prompt must instruct the module to consume question and summary_1 and produce exactly one compact second-hop query field.
input_dict.base_prompt:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, produce the fields `query`.

Required output format and constraints:
- Produce exactly one field named `query`. Its value must be a single short, keyword-style search string (no full sentences, no explanations, no assertions, no user-facing clarifications).
- The query must be terse (preferably <= 12 words) and contain no clause punctuation or sentence grammar (no question marks, no leading "who/what is", no verbs forming assertions).
- Use only tokens that are visible in `question` and/or `summary_1`, except you may insert at most one minimal neutral connector token to target bridging evidence (allowed connector tokens include, but are not limited to: artist, album, director, who directed, biography, introduction, genre, regular season). Do not invent new entity names or facts.
- When the inputs present multiple entities or candidate items that must be linked (for example two titles, two names, or an anchor title plus a candidate), include both entity tokens from the inputs and a single neutral connector token between or adjacent to them to indicate the evidence bridge to retrieve (e.g., "EntityA EntityB director").
- If the question or summary includes structured tokens relevant to the bridge (years, league IDs, edition names), include those tokens plus a concise cue (for example year + league + "regular season") as part of the short query.
- You may perform light normalization of obvious misspellings in entity names or technical terms drawn from the visible inputs (fix clear typos), but do not invent alternative candidate entities or assert corrected identities beyond the token-level fix.
- If it is not possible to form a fully disambiguating query from visible tokens, still emit the best compact retrieval query possible using only visible tokens and an allowed connector; do not ask the user for clarification at this step. (Downstream retrieval will be attempted first; user clarification is allowed only after retrieval fails.)

Produce only the `query` field value (the single short search string) and nothing else.
input_dict.integrated_feedback:
{
  "integrated_feedback": "Queries often drop or fail to include crucial tokens that disambiguate or bridge entities (missing second entity, adjacent qualifiers like service names, years, birth/bio cues, or explicit page titles). The smallest robust fix is to require the terse query to preserve every entity token present and to carry along any nearby disambiguator or attribute token visible in the inputs so retrieval can find the needed bridge.",
  "update_directions": [
    "When multiple entity tokens appear in the inputs, always include every entity token in the single terse query and use at most one neutral connector token to indicate the relation between them.",
    "If a visible disambiguator or attribute token appears adjacent to an entity in the inputs (for example a multiword service name, an explicit page title, a year, or an attribute marker like a birth/producer/service/series cue), include that disambiguator token with the associated entity in the query rather than dropping or truncating it."
  ],
  "preservation_constraints": [
    "Keep the existing single-field output contract: exactly one `query` string.",
    "Maintain terseness and formatting: keyword-style, no sentence grammar or clause punctuation, prefer <=12 words.",
    "Use only tokens visible in `question` and/or `summary_1` (allow at most one additional minimal neutral connector token); do not invent facts or new entity names.",
    "Do not return answers or explanatory text in the query."
  ],
  "edit_recommendations": [
    {
      "action": "clarify",
      "target_behavior": "base rule requiring tokens come only from question/summary_1 and use at most one connector",
      "proposed_change": "When composing the single terse query, if inputs contain multiple distinct entity tokens include every entity token in the query. Place a single neutral connector token between or adjacent to entity tokens when needed to indicate the targeted attribute or relation.",
      "reason": "Many failures stem from omitting one of multiple entities; forcing inclusion preserves the bridge and stays within existing token-source and terseness constraints."
    },
    {
      "action": "clarify",
      "target_behavior": "handling of disambiguating qualifiers and explicit page cues",
      "proposed_change": "If the inputs include an explicit disambiguator or attribute token adjacent to an entity (for example multiword service names, visible years, birth/bio markers, role tokens, or an explicit Fetch page title), include that disambiguator token with the entity in the query. If summary_1 contains an explicit Fetch page title instruction, prefer that exact page-title token sequence as the query value.",
      "reason": "Dropping short disambiguators (multiword service suffixes, years, birth-related words, exact page titles) causes ambiguous retrievals; including them is a minimal, safe change that meaningfully improves retrieval precision."
    }
  ],
  "rejected_local_patterns": [
    "Do not hardcode sample-specific replacements or single-case heuristics (for example \"always replace 'letters' with 'mail'\" or \"always append 'musician' to a specific name\").",
    "Do not add award-claim words or other evaluative tokens as a blanket rule.",
    "Do not create separate one-off rules per question type (birth comparisons, producers, series) that only apply to individual examples."
  ],
  "new_capability_required": false,
  "length_increase_justified": false,
  "generalization_rationale": "Missing or truncated tokens are a recurring, general cause of failed second-hop retrievals. Requiring preservation of all visible entity tokens and any adjacent disambiguator/attribute tokens addresses multiple observed failure modes (dropped entities, lost service qualifiers, missing years, absent 'born' cues, omitted page titles) with a single, input-driven rule that keeps queries terse and token-limited.",
  "generalization_check": "The proposed directions operate on abstract roles (entity token, disambiguator/attribute token, explicit page title, neutral connector) and apply whenever those roles appear in inputs; they are not a list of sample-specific fixes and do not rely on any individual example's proper nouns or phrasing."
}

request.rendered_prompt:
You are rewriting one agent prompt in a fixed multi-agent HotpotQA
pipeline.

You receive only:
1. the current base prompt;
2. one already-synthesized integrated feedback object.

You do not have access to the raw optimization examples. Do not reconstruct
or invent sample-specific examples.

Produce one complete, directly usable shared prompt.

Requirements:
- Preserve the native input/output interface exactly.
- Start from the supplied base prompt and make the smallest coherent update
  justified by the integrated feedback.
- Keep useful base behavior that is not contradicted or superseded.
- Prefer revising, clarifying, replacing, or merging existing instructions
  when they already address the same behavior.
- Add a new instruction when the feedback identifies a genuinely missing
  capability that cannot be expressed clearly by modifying existing text.
- Avoid adding a separate rule block when the change can be integrated into
  the current structure.
- Prompt length may increase when a genuinely new capability is required.
  Any increase should be proportional to the new information, while
  redundant, overlapping, or superseded instructions should be removed or
  merged where practical.
- Preserve multiple rules when they represent genuinely independent
  behaviors, but avoid separate rules for individual examples, answer types,
  lexical patterns, or incidental formatting properties.
- Treat edit recommendations as an edit plan, not as text to append
  verbatim.
- Do not copy optimization terminology into the final prompt.
- Do not mention feedback, training, attribution, batches, examples,
  endpoints, deltas, metrics, or prompt optimization.
- Do not add proper nouns, document titles, answers, locations, literal
  case labels, or sample-specific exception lists.
- Keep the final prompt complete and self-contained.
- Do not refer to an omitted original prompt or unwritten rules.

Condition:
endpoint_delta_custom_signed

Agent:
query

Agent role:
Produce one compact second-hop retrieval query targeting the missing bridge or supporting evidence.

Agent function and pipeline position:
Pipeline position: after summary1 and before second-hop retrieval. Inputs are the original question and the first-hop intermediate state. Functional responsibility: produce one focused retrieval query that is likely to recover the missing evidence needed by the downstream agents. Query quality is determined by evidence recovery, not by whether the query itself resembles or contains the final answer.

Native output contract:
The prompt must instruct the module to consume question and summary_1 and produce exactly one compact second-hop query field.

Base prompt:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, produce the fields `query`.

Required output format and constraints:
- Produce exactly one field named `query`. Its value must be a single short, keyword-style search string (no full sentences, no explanations, no assertions, no user-facing clarifications).
- The query must be terse (preferably <= 12 words) and contain no clause punctuation or sentence grammar (no question marks, no leading "who/what is", no verbs forming assertions).
- Use only tokens that are visible in `question` and/or `summary_1`, except you may insert at most one minimal neutral connector token to target bridging evidence (allowed connector tokens include, but are not limited to: artist, album, director, who directed, biography, introduction, genre, regular season). Do not invent new entity names or facts.
- When the inputs present multiple entities or candidate items that must be linked (for example two titles, two names, or an anchor title plus a candidate), include both entity tokens from the inputs and a single neutral connector token between or adjacent to them to indicate the evidence bridge to retrieve (e.g., "EntityA EntityB director").
- If the question or summary includes structured tokens relevant to the bridge (years, league IDs, edition names), include those tokens plus a concise cue (for example year + league + "regular season") as part of the short query.
- You may perform light normalization of obvious misspellings in entity names or technical terms drawn from the visible inputs (fix clear typos), but do not invent alternative candidate entities or assert corrected identities beyond the token-level fix.
- If it is not possible to form a fully disambiguating query from visible tokens, still emit the best compact retrieval query possible using only visible tokens and an allowed connector; do not ask the user for clarification at this step. (Downstream retrieval will be attempted first; user clarification is allowed only after retrieval fails.)

Produce only the `query` field value (the single short search string) and nothing else.

Return strict JSON only:
{
  "updated_prompt": "complete directly usable shared prompt",
  "clarified_instructions": [
    "existing behavior clarified or narrowed"
  ],
  "merged_instructions": [

  ],

    "incorrect or overly broad behavior replaced"

response:
{
  "updated_prompt": "Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, produce the fields `query`.\n\nAgent role: Produce one compact second-hop retrieval query targeting the missing bridge or supporting evidence.\n\nPipeline position: after summary1 and before second-hop retrieval. Inputs are the original question and the first-hop intermediate state. Functional responsibility: produce one focused retrieval query that is likely to recover the missing evidence needed by the downstream agents. Query quality is determined by evidence recovery, not by whether the query itself resembles or contains the final answer.\n\nNative output contract: The module must consume `question` and `summary_1` and produce exactly one compact second-hop query field named `query`.\n\nRequired output format and constraints:\n- Produce exactly one field named `query`. Its value must be a single short, keyword-style search string (no full sentences, no explanations, no assertions, no user-facing clarifications).\n- The query must be terse (preferably <= 12 words) and contain no clause punctuation or sentence grammar (no question marks, no leading \"who/what is\", no verbs forming assertions).\n- Use only tokens that are visible in `question` and/or `summary_1`, except you may insert at most one minimal neutral connector token to target bridging evidence (allowed connector tokens include, but are not limited to: artist, album, director, who directed, biography, introduction, genre, regular season). Do not invent new entity names or facts.\n- When the inputs present multiple entities or candidate items that must be linked (for example two titles, two names, or an anchor title plus a candidate), include every distinct entity token visible in the inputs in the single terse query and place at most one neutral connector token between or adjacent to them to indicate the targeted relation or attribute (for example: \"EntityA EntityB director\").\n- If a visible disambiguator or attribute token appears adjacent to an entity in the inputs (for example a multiword service name, an explicit page title, a year, or an attribute marker like a birth/producer/service/series cue), include that disambiguator token together with the associated entity in the query rather than dropping or truncating it.\n- If `summary_1` contains an explicit page-title token sequence (for example an instruction-like ExactPageTitle or Fetch page title), prefer that exact page-title token sequence as the query value.\n- When the question or summary includes structured tokens relevant to the bridge (years, league IDs, edition names), include those tokens plus a concise cue (for example year + league + \"regular season\") as part of the short query.\n- You may perform light normalization of obvious misspellings in entity names or technical terms drawn from the visible inputs (fix clear typos), but do not invent alternative candidate entities or assert corrected identities beyond the token-level fix.\n- If it is not possible to form a fully disambiguating query from visible tokens, still emit the best compact retrieval query possible using only visible tokens and an allowed connector; do not ask the user for clarification at this step. (Downstream retrieval will be attempted first; user clarification is allowed only after retrieval fails.)\n\nProduce only the `query` field value (the single short search string) and nothing else.",
  "clarified_instructions": [
    "Require inclusion of every distinct entity token present in the inputs when multiple entities appear, with at most one neutral connector token between or adjacent to them.",
    "Require inclusion of any visible disambiguator or attribute token adjacent to an entity (multiword service names, years, birth/bio markers, role tokens, explicit page-title sequences) instead of dropping or truncating them.",
    "If summary_1 includes an explicit Fetch page-title token sequence, prefer that exact sequence as the query value."
  ],
  "merged_instructions": [
    "Merged the original guidance about using only tokens from question/summary_1 and at most one connector with the new rule to preserve all entity tokens and adjacent disambiguators into a single coherent token-source and composition rule.",
    "Consolidated the earlier examples of connector usage with the requirement to place at most one neutral connector between multiple entities."
  ],
  "replaced_instructions": [
    "Replaced a weaker, implicit allowance to drop or truncate entity-adjacent qualifiers with an explicit rule to include adjacent disambiguator/attribute tokens with their entity."
  ],
  "added_instructions": [
    "Explicitly require that every distinct entity token visible in the inputs be preserved in the single terse query when multiple entities are present.",
    "Explicit instruction to prefer exact page-title token sequences from summary_1 when present."
  ],
  "remaining_conflicts": [],
  "rationale": "Small, targeted edits ensure queries retain all entity tokens and nearby disambiguators visible in the inputs so retrieval can find the bridge. The update preserves the original terse, single-field contract and token-source constraints while reducing failures caused by dropped or truncated qualifiers."
}
